rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they refer to visible ports and features.
Use visible identifiers from the AADL model to define assumptions and guarantees.
generic_rules: Use explicit type declarations in AGREE equations to match AADL data types.
Avoid using AADL property names directly in AGREE expressions.
Write range assumptions using visible feature names and concrete bounds.
Ensure AGREE contracts are placed in valid component type or implementation scopes.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};`.
Assumptions and guarantees must reference valid visible identifiers.
Boolean expressions in AGREE should match the actual data type of the port.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL properties like Period or Deadline directly in AGREE expressions.
Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type contracts.
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers unless explicitly supported by the AADL model.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and timing constraints
